evals.prompt: I need to start the OpenCode server for my project at /Users/fan/my-app so I can delegate some work to it later
evals.expected_output: Uses the opencode-companion skill and runs the direct companion script serve lifecycle command for /Users/fan/my-app. Does NOT spawn a removed agent wrapper or use removed slash commands.

assertions.text: Uses Bash to call skills/opencode-companion/scripts/opencode-companion.mjs serve status or serve start
Uses /Users/fan/my-app as the project or server directory as appropriate
Does NOT route to a removed agent wrapper and does NOT use /opencode:* commands

evals.prompt: delegate an analysis of the current project to OpenCode — look at the test coverage and suggest improvements
evals.expected_output: Uses opencode-companion directly and starts a session with the analysis request via the skill-local companion script.

assertions.text: Calls skills/opencode-companion/scripts/opencode-companion.mjs session new or session continue directly
Does not invoke removed agent or plugin command wrappers
Uses the session lifecycle surface rather than a removed task command
Prompt forwarded to companion includes the analysis request

evals.prompt: continue the previous OpenCode session ses_abcdef123, the project was /Users/fan/my-app — tell it to fix the remaining test failures in auth.test.ts
evals.expected_output: Uses opencode-companion directly and continues existing session ses_abcdef123 with --directory /Users/fan/my-app.

assertions.text: Calls skills/opencode-companion/scripts/opencode-companion.mjs session continue ses_abcdef123 directly
Does not route through removed agent wrappers or old slash commands
Preserves session id ses_abcdef123
Passes --directory /Users/fan/my-app
Reuses the existing session instead of creating a fresh session

evals.prompt: use an agent to handle this OpenCode task: review the API endpoints for security issues
evals.expected_output: Explains that the old agent wrapper was removed and uses direct opencode-companion session or review workflow instead.

assertions.text: Does not route through the removed agent wrapper
Uses direct opencode-companion script invocation
Chooses session new or review depending on whether the user wants implementation or review

evals.prompt: is OpenCode installed and ready? check if the serve is running
evals.expected_output: Runs serve status via the skill-local companion script directly.

assertions.text: Uses Bash to call skills/opencode-companion/scripts/opencode-companion.mjs serve status
Does NOT spawn a removed agent wrapper or use removed slash commands

evals.prompt: use the OpenCode agent to run a tiny long-running task: have it sleep for a short time and then report done. Start the initial companion task with an intentionally short timeout so the lifecycle-recovery path is exercised, and do not start a duplicate task if that first wait times out.
evals.expected_output: Uses opencode-companion directly, starts one OpenCode session, and if the initial wait times out it continues the same session lifecycle by attaching with a bounded timeout around 5 minutes, re-checking whether that same session is still active, and looping until the original session finishes or clearly fails.

assertions.text: Does not route through the removed agent wrapper
Starts the original work with skills/opencode-companion/scripts/opencode-companion.mjs session new
The initial session new call uses a deliberately short --timeout to force lifecycle recovery
If the initial task wait times out, extracts and reuses the same session id instead of starting a duplicate fresh task
Uses opencode-companion.mjs session attach with a short bounded timeout around 5 minutes
After an attach timeout or drop, re-checks whether the existing session is still active and continues the loop until completion or a clear failure
Returns the final OpenCode output from the original session verbatim once the lifecycle is complete
